chief_complaint: A 59-year-old Han female patient presented with generalized abdominal pain without obvious cause for 3 days.
history: The patient was admitted to the hospital for abdominal pain. Initial imaging revealed a left ureteral stone and hydronephrosis, prompting ureteral stent placement. Despite antispasmodics, analgesics, and stent insertion, her pain persisted. The patient had a history of diabetes.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the case report.
labs: Laboratory tests confirmed diabetic ketoacidosis. Initial imaging revealed a left ureteral stone and hydronephrosis.
differentials: Abdominal pain caused by ureteral stones
correct_ranking: Abdominal pain caused by ureteral stones
